data.body: Plan-review advisory triage (PASS, 0 blocking, 19 advisory). Actionable resolved in design: (A adv1/3) verify(kind, envelope, trust_root): envelope=dsse.Envelope, trust_root=caller-supplied per-scheme trust material (opaque). (B adv2/6/7) namespace = domain-separation string the policy pins and passes to the scheme; test asserts scheme invoked with policy-pinned namespace. (C adv4/5) DELIBERATE split: registry pins security-critical {scheme,namespace} (the dispatch key, never read from envelope -> anti alg-confusion); trust material is caller-supplied (which keys to trust is a deployment concern). (D adv9/11/12) POLICY is a hardcoded module-level dict (source-pinned, NOT config/env-loaded - that IS the security property); schemes register into a module dict via register_scheme(). (E adv10) unknown kind -> fail closed (raise/fail verdict), tested. (F adv13) added corrupt-signature verify-failure test via a fake scheme. (adv8) file_impact set. (adv17) resolved: 9fd5 attest package now committed. JUSTIFIED-INVALID (severity none, impact 0.0): adv14/15/16/18/19 argue premature-abstraction/rule-of-three/already-solved -> the decision to build ONE shared asymmetric substrate (vs duplicating security-critical crypto across the identity + op-cert epics) was made and plan-reviewed at the EPIC level (2fd4 Context); this leaf implements that approved decision, not re-litigate it. Re-signing after file_impact change.
